summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a 'cardioid' in polar graphing?

A cardioid is a special limacon where the curve has a cusp at the pole, resembling a heart shape.

What is a 'rose curve'?

Rose curves produce symmetrical petal patterns, with n petals if n is odd and 2n petals if n is even.

What is a 'limacon'?

Limacons (from French for 'snail') are a family of curves that includes cardioids as a special case.

What is a 'lemniscate'?

A lemniscate has the shape of an infinity symbol or figure-eight.

What is 'symmetry about the pole'?

Symmetry about the pole (origin) means the curve is unchanged under 180° rotation.

What is an 'Archimedean spiral'?

In an Archimedean spiral, the distance from the center increases linearly with the angle.

What is a 'dimpled limacon'?

When a > b (but a < 2b) in r = a + b cos θ, the limacon has a dimple (indentation) but no loop.

A garden designer creates a flower bed shaped like a 4-petal rose r = 6 sin(2θ). What is the maximum petal length?

The maximum r = 6 occurs when sin(2θ) = 1, so each petal extends 6 units from the center.

A sprinkler has a coverage pattern r = 5(1 + cos θ) meters. What is the total diameter of coverage along the polar axis direction?

At θ = 0: r = 10 (maximum). At θ = π: r = 0. Total reach along polar axis = 10 m.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: A wind turbine blade traces a cardioid pattern r = 2(1 + cos θ) as it flexes. At θ = π/3, the blade tip distance from the hub is:

r = 2(1 + cos(π/3)) = 2(1 + 1/2) = 2(3/2) = 3.

Example: A sprinkler has a coverage pattern r = 5(1 + cos θ) meters. What is the total diameter of coverage along the polar axis direction?

At θ = 0: r = 10 (maximum). At θ = π: r = 0. Total reach along polar axis = 10 m.
